title: Mechanical Engineer — Nuclear Target Systems
about: ASPL Fusion (Agnira Sanlayan Pvt. Ltd.) is an applied plasma and neutron-systems company working on accelerator-based BNCT, domestic medical-isotope production, and industrial neutron applications, within established Indian regulatory and safety frameworks. The target and moderator assemblies sit at the centre of the neutron source — high heat flux, vacuum, radiation environment, precision moderation requirements all at once.
description: Design and build the mechanical systems at the heart of ASPL's neutron source: the lithium target assembly with micro-channel cooling, the Beam Shaping Assembly (BSA) moderator structure, and the integration with biological shielding.
responsibilities: Design the target assembly with micro-channel cooling (managing ~5–30 kW beam power on target)
Design and specify biological shielding for the accelerator room
Create engineering drawings (SolidWorks / CATIA) for fabrication
Coordinate with accelerator physicists on beam–target geometry
Support thermal-hydraulic analysis (CFD for target cooling)
Manage fabrication and assembly of mechanical components
requirements: M.Tech in Mechanical Engineering (thermal-fluid systems, nuclear components, or precision engineering)
Experience with high-heat-flux component design (target systems, heat exchangers, or similar)
Proficiency in SolidWorks, CATIA, or equivalent CAD
CFD / FEA simulation experience (ANSYS Fluent, COMSOL, or equivalent)
Understanding of vacuum systems and ultra-clean manufacturing
preferred: Prior experience in nuclear or accelerator environments
Publications or patents on thermal-management or precision-mechanical systems
benefits: Co-authorship on ASPL publications and patent filings (the default for technical roles)
Conference travel support (ANS, IEEE NPSS, IPAC, ASTRO, IAEA technical meetings and equivalent)
Direct mentorship from Prof. Prabhat Ranjan and Prof. Y.C. Saxena, plus weekly working-group technical syncs
Founding-team scope: shape your domain end-to-end as part of a small team scaling up
Core collaboration hours 10:00–18:00 IST with flexibility outside that window
